info.title: SubscriptionClient
info.description: Subscription client provides an interface to create and manage Azure subscriptions programmatically.

parameters.name: aliasName
parameters.description: AliasName is the name for the subscription creation request. Note that this is not the same as subscription name and this doesn’t have any other lifecycle need beyond the request for subscription creation.

parameters.name: aliasName
parameters.description: AliasName is the name for the subscription creation request. Note that this is not the same as subscription name and this doesn’t have any other lifecycle need beyond the request for subscription creation.

Location.description: The Location header contains the URL where the status of the long running operation can be checked.

Retry-After.description: The Retry-After header can indicate how long the client should wait before polling the operation status.

parameters.name: aliasName
parameters.description: AliasName is the name for the subscription creation request. Note that this is not the same as subscription name and this doesn’t have any other lifecycle need beyond the request for subscription creation.

get.description: Lists all of the available Microsoft.Subscription API operations.
Get the subscription tenant policy for the user's tenant.
Get the subscription tenant policy for the user's tenant.

put.description: Create or Update Subscription tenant policy for user's tenant.

get.description: Get the status of the pending Microsoft.Subscription API operations.

parameters.name: operationId
parameters.description: The operation ID, which can be found from the Location field in the generate recommendation response header.

202.description: The request has been accepted for processing, but processing has not yet completed.

Location.description: The Location header contains the URL where the status of the long running operation can be checked.

Retry-After.description: The Retry-After header can indicate how long the client should wait before polling the operation status.

post.description: The operation to accept Subscription Changed Request

204.description: There is no content to send for this request, but the headers may be useful.

Location.description: The Location header contains the URL where the status of the long running operation can be checked.

Retry-After.description: The Retry-After header can indicate how long the client should wait before polling the operation status.

post.description: The operation for Acceptor to view the accepted request
The operation to cancel a subscription

The operation to view Initiator Subscription Changed Request

delete.description: The operation to delete Initiator Subscription Changed Request

The operation to rename a subscription

AcceptOwnership.description: The accept ownership state of the resource.

AcceptOwnershipRequest.description: The parameters required to accept subscription ownership.

displayName.description: The friendly name of the subscription.

managementGroupId.description: Management group Id for the subscription.

acceptOwnershipState.description: The accept ownership state of the resource.

BillingAccountPoliciesResponseProperties.description: Put billing account policies response properties.

serviceTenants.description: Service tenant for the billing account.

allowTransfers.description: Determine if the transfers are allowed for the billing account

CanceledSubscriptionId.description: The ID of the canceled subscription

subscriptionId.description: The ID of the canceled subscription

ChangeDirectoryOperationStatus.description: Status of the subscription transfer operation.

values.name: Initialized
values.description: The subscription transfer request has been created and is waiting for acceptance by the destination tenant.

values.name: InProgress
values.description: The subscription transfer request has been accepted by the destination tenant and the transfer is in progress.

values.name: Completed
values.description: The subscription transfer has been completed and is now owned by the destination tenant.

EnabledSubscriptionId.description: The ID of the subscriptions that is being enabled

subscriptionId.description: The ID of the subscriptions that is being enabled

value.description: The GetTenantPolicyResponse items on this page

nextLink.description: The link to the next page of items

Provisioning.description: The provisioning state of the resource.

ProvisioningState.description: The provisioning state of the resource.

PutAliasRequest.description: The parameters required to create a new subscription.

managementGroupId.description: Management group Id for the subscription.

displayName.description: The friendly name of the subscription.

workload.description: The workload type of the subscription. It can be either Production or DevTest.

subscriptionId.description: This parameter can be used to create alias for existing subscription Id

blockSubscriptionsLeavingTenant.description: Blocks the leaving of subscriptions from user's tenant.

blockSubscriptionsIntoTenant.description: Blocks the entering of subscriptions into user's tenant.

exemptedPrincipals.description: List of user objectIds that are exempted from the set subscription tenant policies for the user's tenant.

subscriptionId.description: The ID of the subscriptions that is being renamed

nextLink.description: The link (url) to the next page of results.

displayName.description: The display name of the subscription.

provisioningState.description: The provisioning state of the resource.

acceptOwnershipUrl.description: Url to accept ownership of the subscription.

acceptOwnershipState.description: The accept ownership state of the resource.

workload.description: The workload type of the subscription. It can be either Production or DevTest.

subscriptionLink.description: The link to the new subscription. Use this link to check the status of subscription creation operation.

SubscriptionName.description: The new name of the subscription.

TargetDirectoryListResult.description: Subscription Response to list out Changed Target Directory.

value.description: The list of subscription response to changed target directory.

nextLink.description: The link (url) to the next page of results.

TargetDirectoryRequest.description: Subscription Request for Changed Target Directory.

TargetDirectoryRequestProperties.description: Properties of subscription Request for Changed Target Directory.

destinationOwnerId.description: The destination OwnerId, can be object id or email address

destinationTenantId.description: The destination Tenant id where subscription needs to be accepted

TargetDirectoryResult.description: Subscription Response for Changed Target Directory.

properties.description: Subscription Changed Target Directory response properties.

TargetDirectoryResultProperties.description: Properties of subscription Response for Changed Target Directory.

destinationTenantId.description: Destination Tenant Id where Subscription will be accepted.

destinationOwnerId.description: Destination Owner Id where Subscription will be accepted.

subscriptionId.description: The id of the subscription being transferred.

createdDate.description: The UTC date and time when the transfer request was created

acceptedDate.description: The UTC date and time when the transfer request was accepted

sourceOwnerEmail.description: The email address of the user who initiated the transfer request. If the request was generated by a Service Principal, this field may be null.

sourceOwnerId.description: The object id of the user who initiated the transfer request.

sourceTenantId.description: The id of the tenant where the subscription originally resided.

status.description: Status of the subscription transfer operation.

blockSubscriptionsLeavingTenant.description: Blocks the leaving of subscriptions from user's tenant.

blockSubscriptionsIntoTenant.description: Blocks the entering of subscriptions into user's tenant.

exemptedPrincipals.description: List of user objectIds that are exempted from the set subscription tenant policies for the user's tenant.

Workload.description: The workload type of the subscription. It can be either Production or DevTest.
